input_row.title: Ensure 'Python version' is currently supported (if in use)
input_row.remediation: Note: No action is required if Python is not in use. Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services or Function App. 2. Click the name of a function app. 3. Under Settings, click Configuration. 4. In the General settings pane, for a Stack of Python, set the Python Version to a currently supported release. 5. Click Save. 6. Click Continue. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each function app requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI Run the following command to list supported runtimes: az functionapp list-runtimes For each function app requiring remediation, run the following command with the appropriate parameters to update the Python version: az functionapp config set --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <app- name> --linux-fx-version "PYTHON|<version>" Default Value: The version is selected during creation. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/app-service/configure- common#configure-language-stack-settings 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- posture-vulnerability-management#pv-7-rapidly-and-automatically-remediate- software-vulnerabilities 3. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v3- posture-vulnerability-management#pv-3-establish-secure-configurations-for- compute-resources 4. https://devguide.python.org/versions/ 5. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/functionapp 6. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/az.functions/get- azfunctionapp CIS Controls:

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_functionapp_python_version_supported_checked

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

- **Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to use:**
  - To list supported Python runtimes for Azure Function Apps: 
    ```bash
    az functionapp list-runtimes
    ```
  - To get the configuration of each Function App:
    ```bash
    az functionapp show --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name>
    ```
  - To update the Python version for each Function App:
    ```bash
    az functionapp config set --resource-group <resource-group-name> --name <function-app-name> --linux-fx-version "PYTHON|<supported_version>"
    ```

- **Specific resources to enumerate:** 
  - Function Apps located in the subscriptions or resource groups being audited.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Confirm the `linux-fx-version` setting for Python in the General settings pane matches the currently supported version returned by the `az functionapp list-runtimes` command.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** The `linux-fx-version` should be set to a version that is included in the list of supported runtimes from the `az functionapp list-runtimes` command.
  - **Fail:** If the `linux-fx-version` is not set to a supported version or if Python is in use but set to an unsupported version.

- **Example API endpoint or CLI command:**
  - Example command to check a specific Function App:
    ```bash
    az functionapp show --resource-group myResourceGroup --name myFunctionApp
    ``` 

The implementation of this automated approach will ensure that the Python version used in Azure Function Apps is compliant with the requirement to use currently supported versions.
